content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 30, 1991

Joe Camicia
City Council Member
City of Alameda
2263 Santa Clara Avenue, #315
Alameda, CA 94501

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No.: A-91-341

Dear Mr. Camicia:

You have requested advice regarding the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act ("the "Act").1/ This letter will
address the questions raised in your letter of July 3, 1991 and in
our telephone conversation on July 12, 1991.

QUESTIONS

1. What are your reporting obligations if you change your
committee name from "Committee to Elect Joe Camicia" to "Friends
of Joe Camicia?"

2. When are you required to disclose activities related to
your June 20, 1991 fundraiser?

CONCLUSIONS

1. You must amend the committee's Statement of Organization
(Form 410) within 10 days of changing your committee name.

2. You must report the fundraiser activities on your 1991
semi-annual campaign statement (Form 490) due July 31, 1991.

_________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Camicia, Joe
Page 2


FACTS

You were recently elected as a Councilmember for the City of
Santa Clara. During the campaign you established a committee
called "Committee to Elect Joe Camicia." You now wish to change
the name of that committee to "Friends of Joe Camicia." You
recently filed a new candidate bank account form 502 under
the name of "Friends of Joe Camicia" for officeholder expenses.
During our conversation on July 12, 1991, you indicated that you
no longer wanted to open a new campaign bank account but instead
wanted to use your bank account established for seeking office for
officeholder expenses. After your election, your committee held a
fund-raiser on June 20, 1991.

ANALYSIS

The Act requires candidates that receive or spend $1,000 or
more in a calendar year to file a Statement of Organization (Form
410). (Section 84102.) Whenever there is a change in any of the
information contained in a Statement of Organization, such as a
change of a committee's name, an amended Form 410 must be filed
within 10 days to reflect the change. (Section 84103.) In
addition, the Act requires candidates to establish one campaign
bank account for receiving contributions and making expenditures
associated with seeking and holding an office. (Section 85201.)
Therefore, under the Act, you may use the bank account established
for your election to city council for officeholder expenses. You
indicated during our telephone conversation that you now wish to
terminate the new bank account. A Form 502 should be filed with
our office to indicate termination of the account.

In addition, the Act requires candidate controlled committees
to file semi-annual campaign statements. (Section 84200.) All
contributions received and expenditures made in connection with a
fundraiser held on June 20, 1991, are required to be disclosed on
the semi-annual statement due July 31, 1991. The statement must
cover the period beginning the day after the closing date of the
last campaign statement filed through June 30, 1991. If no
previous statement has been filed, the period begins January 1,
1991. (Section 82046.)

Camicia, Joe
Page 3


Enclosed are Forms 410 and 502, which you should use to amend
the current Statement of Organization, and to terminate the
campaign bank account. Please do not hesitate to contact me at
(916) 322-5662 if you have additional questions.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures
